title: Delete Source Object and Mapping Metadata
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to delete a source object and all its associated mapping metadata from a Salesforce object's mapping screen, so that I can clean up incorrect or outdated migration configurations.
description:
Add the ability to delete a source object from the mapping screen. When a user wants to remove all mapping data and source object metadata from a specific Salesforce object, they should have an option to delete it. This removes the source object entry and all associated field mappings.

This is useful when a migration configuration needs to be reset or when a source object was added in error.
acceptance_criteria: Delete option is available for source objects on the mapping screen
Deleting a source object removes all associated field mappings
Deleting a source object removes the source object metadata
Main data table labels and statuses update to reflect removed mappings
Confirmation prompt is shown before deletion
ux_changes: New delete option on the mapping screen for source objects. Removes the source object and all associated mapping data with a confirmation prompt.
middleware_changes: Cascade delete logic that removes the source object record and all associated field mapping entries from the database.
data_model_changes: Cascade deletion of source object metadata and associated mapping records from the mapping tables.
implementation_notes: Cascade delete ensures all related mapping data is cleaned up when a source object is removed.
comment_summary: Developer implemented and deployed to development for review.
